heritage.title: Winslow Homer (1836-1910) : Watercolor on Paper, Coastal Maine Scene
heritage.description: Watercolor on paper laid on board, signed lower right 'Winslow Homer'. A dramatic coastal Maine scene depicting crashing surf against rocky headlands with two figures in the distance. Ex. collection of a prominent New England family, documented provenance to 1924. Condition: excellent, minor toning to margins, unexamined outside of frame.
heritage.scraper_notes: Served from seeded fallback : Heritage returned 403; flagged for API arrangement.

heritage.title: Tiffany Studios (1893-1933) : Favrile Glass Wisteria Table Lamp
heritage.description: Leaded glass and patinated bronze, shade unsigned. Wisteria design in rich mauves, lavenders and greens with a mottled blue sky ground. Original base with verdigris patina, pad feet. Shade diameter: 18 in. (45.7 cm); height overall: 27 in. (68.6 cm). Provenance: private New York estate.

heritage.title: Gustav Stickley (1858-1942) : Oak Library Table, Model No. 653
heritage.description: Quartersawn white oak, original medium brown finish, lower shelf, through-tenon construction. Red decal signature, paper label intact. 29 × 48 × 30 in. (73.7 × 121.9 × 76.2 cm). Minor surface wear consistent with age; no repairs or refinishing. Provenance: private Illinois collection acquired 1987.

heritage.title: Paul Revere Jr. (1735-1818) : Sterling Silver Sauce Boat, Boston, c.1780
heritage.description: Silver sauce boat, engraved armorial crest, shaped rim, flying scroll handle, three pad feet. Maker's mark 'REVERE' in rectangle, clearly struck. Weight: 6.25 troy oz (194.4 g); length: 7.5 in. (19 cm). Fine original condition, light surface patina. Ex. private Boston collection, by descent.

heritage.title: Chinese Export Porcelain Famille Rose Punch Bowl, Qianlong Period (1736-1796)
heritage.description: Large punch bowl with hand-painted floral medallions and butterfly border in famille rose enamels. Interior: central peony spray. Exterior: alternating panels of flowers and landscapes. Diameter: 15.75 in. (40 cm); height: 6.5 in. (16.5 cm). Old hairline crack to interior, professionally stabilized. Ex. English country house collection.

heritage.title: Art Deco Silvered Bronze Figure of Diana the Huntress, French, c.1925
heritage.description: Silvered bronze on an onyx and gilt-bronze base. Diana stands in classical contrapposto, bow drawn, with hunting dog at her feet. Signed 'A. Bouraine' on the base. Height overall: 22 in. (55.9 cm). Fine original patina, no repairs. Base: 4 × 8 × 3 in. (10.2 × 20.3 × 7.6 cm). Provenance: Paris estate.

heritage.title: Norman Rockwell (1894-1978) : Original Study for 'The Shiner', c.1953
heritage.description: Oil on canvas, pencil underdrawing visible under raking light. Depicts a young girl with a black eye sitting outside the principal's office, grinning proudly. Signed lower right 'Norman Rockwell'. 18 × 14 in. (45.7 × 35.6 cm). Accompanied by a COA from the Norman Rockwell Museum, Stockbridge, MA. Published: Saturday Evening Post, May 23, 1953.

bonhams.title: A Victorian Diamond and Sapphire Brooch, c.1880
